episode.title: Something Is Missing
episode.context: A woman in her 30s who craves deep, weird, real conversation — but every date is stuck in small talk. She had it once with her ex and can't find it again. Based on a real Reddit post from r/AskWomenOver30 with 719 upvotes.
episode.art_style: Photorealistic cinematic footage, warm golden hour tones for memory scenes, cooler muted tones for present-day dates. Shallow depth of field, soft natural lighting, intimate framing
episode.narrator_style: Female voice, early 30s, warm but slightly frustrated. Articulate and self-aware. Not whiny — wistful. Like someone journaling out loud.

restaurant_table.name: Restaurant table
restaurant_table.description: A small two-person table at a casual restaurant. White plates, wine glasses, a candle between them. Hands visible but no faces. Present-day scenes — cool, slightly sterile lighting.

golden_memory.name: Memory scene
golden_memory.description: A cozy living room or rooftop at golden hour. Two silhouettes close together, animated in conversation. Warm amber light, lens flare, soft focus. Everything glows. This is the past — the one that worked.

wine_glass.name: Wine glass
wine_glass.description: A glass of red wine on a table, half-full. Light refracts through it. A hand nearby but not holding it.

sentences: I really value having stimulating, deeper conversations.
I love being able to have some humorous, slightly weird banter.
scenes.video_description: Golden hour light streaming through a window onto a cozy couch. Two silhouettes sitting close, leaning toward each other, mid-laugh. Warm amber tones, lens flare, soft focus. Shot through a doorframe as if peering into a private moment. This is a memory.
scenes.camera_style: Static shot through doorframe, warm intimate framing, shallow depth of field

sentences: My ex and I — the first time we talked, we talked for five hours straight.
scenes.video_description: A rooftop at sunset. Two people sitting on the edge, silhouetted against a pink-orange sky. Their body language is close, animated — gesturing, laughing. No faces visible. The city sprawls below, soft and out of focus. Golden warm light everywhere.
scenes.camera_style: Wide shot of two silhouettes on rooftop, slow push-in, golden backlight

sentences: I could be myself around him.
scenes.video_description: Close-up of two hands on a table, fingers intertwined loosely over scattered papers or a book. Warm candlelight. The hands are relaxed, comfortable — this is easy. Soft focus background of a dimly lit room.
scenes.camera_style: Close-up on hands, static, warm intimate lighting, gentle flicker from candle

sentences: Dating now as a single person has made me really miss that.
scenes.video_description: Hard cut to cooler tones. A restaurant table with two place settings. The lighting is harsher — overhead fluorescent mixed with a small candle. A woman's hands are in her lap under the table, fidgeting. The man across is just a shoulder and sleeve. Everything feels stiffer.
scenes.camera_style: Medium shot of table from the side, abrupt tonal shift from warm to cool, static

sentences: Most conversations are on the small talk side.
Discussing normal life things without much banter or deeper conversations or weirdness.
scenes.video_description: A wine glass sitting on a white tablecloth. A hand reaches for it, takes a polite sip, sets it back down. Across the table, another hand mirrors the gesture. Mechanical, polite. The candle between them flickers but the scene feels cold. Slightly desaturated.
scenes.camera_style: Close-up tracking from one glass to the other, slow lateral movement, clinical mood

sentences: I feel that pressure again to be a normal boring adult.
scenes.video_description: A woman's hand resting on a restaurant table, fingers tapping once. Across from her, a man's hand gestures vaguely while talking — we can't hear him. Her fingers stop tapping. She picks up her glass. The moment is endured, not enjoyed.
scenes.camera_style: Close-up on her hand, rack focus to his gesturing hand across the table, back to hers

sentences: I've tried to be more playful and initiate banter.
But they just laugh or don't get it — and then nothing.
scenes.video_description: A moment in a different restaurant — warmer but still muted. A woman's hands make an animated gesture over the table. Across from her, a man's posture is still, polite. A brief pause. Then he reaches for the menu. The energy dies. Shot from the side, catching the mismatch in body language.
scenes.camera_style: Medium side shot of two people at table, focus on contrasting body language, static

sentences: It just seems to fall flat.
scenes.video_description: An empty wine glass on a table. The bill folder sits between the place settings. Two chairs are still occupied but the energy is gone. Overhead shot looking straight down at the table — the geometry is symmetrical, orderly, lifeless.
scenes.camera_style: Overhead bird's-eye shot of table, static, symmetrical composition

sentences: With ninety percent of the people I meet
it just feels like something is missing.
scenes.video_description: A montage of quick cuts: different restaurant tables, different hands, different wine glasses — all slightly different settings but the same emotional tone. Each lasts 1-2 seconds. Cool, muted, repetitive. The sameness is the point.
scenes.camera_style: Quick cuts between 4-5 similar table compositions, each slightly different but emotionally identical

sentences: Unfortunately my ex was incredibly emotionally immature.
So that's why he didn't work out.
scenes.video_description: Brief flash back to golden hour tones — the rooftop silhouettes from scene 2. But now the shot is slightly further away, and one of the silhouettes is standing up, moving away. The golden light is fading. Bittersweet.
scenes.camera_style: Wide shot callback to scene 2, one figure separating, warm light dimming

sentences: But it's been a struggle not to romanticize what we had.
scenes.video_description: Back to present. A woman walking alone on a city street at evening, seen from behind. She passes a restaurant window where a couple is laughing inside. She glances at them briefly, keeps walking. Cool evening light, warm window glow.
scenes.camera_style: Tracking shot from behind, she passes the lit window, camera holds on the window as she walks out of frame

scenes.video_description: Fade to black. Silence. Then white text appears: 'What if your match already knew what makes you light up?' Below: 'Thumos — discover your soul, find your someone.'
scenes.camera_style: Fade to black, text fade-in, hold for 3 seconds
